steps.title: Use tangent's period
steps.content:
Tangent repeats every $\pi$ radians. Rewrite the angle by separating out a whole-number multiple of $\pi$.
$$\frac{110\pi}{3}=36\pi+\frac{2\pi}{3}$$
$$\tan\frac{110\pi}{3}=\tan\frac{2\pi}{3}$$

steps.title: Evaluate the angle
steps.content:
The angle $\frac{2\pi}{3}$ is in quadrant II with reference angle $\frac{\pi}{3}$. Tangent is negative in quadrant II, and $\tan\frac{\pi}{3}=\sqrt{3}$.
$$\tan\frac{2\pi}{3}=-\sqrt{3}$$
The answer is A.
